quick_facts.label: Typical employer setting
quick_facts.value: Assistant Town Planner posts occur in state Town & Country Planning departments, local-self-government planning wings, and planning/development authorities; the exact statutory powers depend on the state or authority.

quick_facts.label: B.Plan relevance
quick_facts.value: A Bachelor of Planning can be a direct-entry qualification where the applicable service rules accept a Bachelor Degree in Planning; eligibility must be checked against the particular state/UT recruitment notification.

quick_facts.label: Recruitment routes
quick_facts.value: Recruitment can be through direct recruitment, promotion/transfer, or deputation depending on the service rules. For example, Odisha specifies direct recruitment or deputation, while a Kerala notification documents a departmental-quota route.

quick_facts.label: Core work
quick_facts.value: Official planning-authority disclosures associate the role with master/development-plan work, development control under zoning regulations, technical advice, and—in some authorities—building-plan or planning-proposal processing.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: Pay is state- and rules-specific. A Manipur Town Planning Department disclosure updated in April 2025 listed Assistant Town Planner at Pay Level 9 with pay band ₹43,300, while a Kerala departmental-quota notice dated December 31, 2024 listed ₹55,200–₹1,15,300.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
paragraphs: The role supports statutory planning and regulated development rather than simply producing layouts. In an Odisha planning-unit disclosure, services connected with an Assistant Town Planner include preparing and implementing master-plan schemes, enforcing development control through zoning regulations, advising revenue authorities on land-use conversion within the master-plan area, and giving technical advice to other agencies.

A broader state department example from Himachal Pradesh includes preparation of development, sectoral and village plans; planning infrastructure; assisting people and government agencies with planning permission; and approving planning proposals or issuing related no-objection certificates. The exact signing, approval, and enforcement authority is determined by the employing department’s legislation, delegation, and post level.
bullets: Prepare, scrutinize, or contribute technical inputs to master, development, sectoral, or area plans.
Assess proposals against land-use plans, zoning regulations, development-control rules, and applicable planning permissions.
Coordinate technical inputs with revenue, engineering, architecture, and other public agencies; some establishments also require site visits and public-facing correspondence.
Support statutory processes such as citizen consultation, objections and suggestions on plans, and processing files upward for departmental approval.

sections.heading: Entry routes after B.Plan
paragraphs: A B.Plan is a relevant immediate route, but it is not a nationwide automatic eligibility rule. Odisha’s Municipal Planning Service rules specify direct recruitment to Assistant Town Planner for an Associate Member of the Institute of Town Planners, India, a Bachelor Degree in Planning, or a postgraduate planning degree from a recognised university; the same rules also require basic computer knowledge.

Other jurisdictions set different combinations of acceptable qualifications. For example, Kerala’s December 31, 2024 Assistant Town Planner departmental-quota notification accepted degrees in Planning/Town and Country Planning/Regional Planning/City Planning/Urban Planning or equivalent, and also listed Civil Engineering, Architecture, and Physical Planning degrees. Candidates should therefore treat the vacancy notification and the applicable recruitment rules—not a generic job title—as definitive.

Recruitment may be conducted through a State Public Service Commission or other notified state/UT process. Read the advertisement for reservation category, age and domicile requirements, qualification-equivalence rules, experience requirements, examination/interview stages, and document-verification instructions.
bullets: Complete a recognised B.Plan and retain degree, marksheets, identity, category/domicile, and other eligibility documents required by the recruiting authority.
Track official state PSC, Town & Country Planning, Urban Development, Local Self Government, and development-authority recruitment pages.
Match the degree title and recognition status exactly to the advertisement; where equivalence is claimed, obtain the evidence requested by the recruiter.
Do not assume that every ATP post is open recruitment: some are departmental quota, promotion, transfer, or deputation posts.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: Planning-rule literacy is central: official descriptions connect the work with master plans, zoning and land-use conformity, development control, planning permissions, and statutory planning acts or municipal bye-laws. A candidate should be able to interpret a plan and translate its provisions into a clear technical note or proposal assessment.

Basic computer knowledge is an explicit eligibility requirement in Odisha’s direct-recruitment rule. Departmental workflows also show Assistant Town Planners working with Associate Town Planners, engineers, draughtsmen, research staff, and administrative sections, then processing matters upward for approval; this makes accurate documentation, coordination, and clear written communication practically important.
bullets: Land-use, zoning, development-control, and master-plan interpretation.
Computer-based drafting, mapping, data handling, and orderly digital file/document preparation.
Technical note writing, proposal scrutiny, and record-based justification of recommendations.
Coordination with engineering, architecture, revenue, legal, and administrative colleagues; respectful communication with applicants and the public.

sections.heading: Workplace and operating context
paragraphs: The post can be based at a state directorate, divisional or sub-divisional town-planning office, local-government planning wing, or a development/planning authority. Himachal Pradesh’s departmental structure lists Assistant Town Planners at headquarters and divisional/sub-divisional field offices, alongside planning officers, engineers, draughtsmen, survey staff, and administrative personnel.

The work mixes desk-based technical scrutiny with field and stakeholder activity. In Manipur’s Town Planning Department, Assistant Town Planners support the wing handling master plans, land-use-conformity checks, and state flagship schemes; files move upward through planning leadership for approval, with technical support from draughtsmen and other staff. Public consultation can also form part of master-plan preparation.
bullets: Desk work: maps, plans, regulations, application files, technical correspondence, and digital records.
Interdisciplinary work: planners, engineers, architects, draughtsmen, surveyors, revenue officials, and administrators.
Field/public work: site-related inputs where assigned, coordination with agencies, and planning consultations or responses to applicants.

sections.heading: Progression
paragraphs: Career ladders are controlled by state service rules. Odisha’s Municipal Planning Service rules place Assistant Town Planner below Associate City Planner and specify at least seven years of Assistant Town Planner service for promotion to that post. Rajasthan’s Town Planning Rules similarly show Assistant Town Planner as the feeder post for Deputy Town Planner, with five years’ experience specified for that promotion in the published rule table.

Progression is therefore usually based on the sanctioned hierarchy, qualifying service, departmental rules, vacancy position, and any required selection process—not solely on years worked. Because titles differ across states, candidates should read the current rules for the department they intend to join.
bullets: Potential next roles may include Associate City Planner, Deputy Town Planner, or comparable state-specific planning posts.
Build a record of statutory-plan work, development-control scrutiny, technical coordination, and compliant file processing.
Check the governing recruitment/promotion rules for minimum service, departmental examinations, and vacancy-based promotion conditions.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide Assistant Town Planner salary. Pay is fixed by the employing state/UT or authority’s pay rules and can differ materially by cadre and recruitment channel. As dated reference points, Manipur’s Town Planning Department disclosure, updated April 2025, lists Assistant Town Planner at Pay Level 9 and ₹43,300; Kerala PSC’s December 31, 2024 departmental-quota notification lists a scale of ₹55,200–₹1,15,300.

These are published pay references, not a portable salary promise or an estimate of take-home pay. Before applying, use the specific current advertisement and applicable pay-revision/allowance orders to confirm basic pay, level or scale, allowances, probation terms, and whether the vacancy is regular, contractual, departmental quota, or deputation.
bullets: Treat compensation figures as dated, employer-specific context.
Verify the current notification’s pay level/scale and the state’s applicable allowance and pay-revision rules.
Distinguish basic pay or pay band from gross and take-home monthly remuneration.
